title: The Docket - Week 1
subject: The AI clause that just voided a $2M deal — and 3 more stories you missed
preheader: Plus: the doc-diff tool every litigator needs right now
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of March 25, 2026

Welcome to the inaugural issue of The Docket — your Wednesday briefing on everything happening at the intersection of AI and legal practice. Let’s get to it.

📄 The $2M Clause Nobody Read

A mid-size firm in Chicago disclosed this week that an AI-drafted force majeure clause in a vendor agreement contained language so broad it was triggered by any supply chain delay — including ones caused by the client’s own subsidiaries. The deal unwound. The lesson: AI can draft fast, but clause-level review isn’t optional. Try our free Clause Checker before your next review cycle.

🤖 Thomson Reuters Acquires Another AI Startup

TR quietly picked up a 40-person NLP startup focused on deposition summarization. This is their fourth AI acquisition in 14 months. The consolidation play is accelerating, and smaller legal AI vendors should be paying attention to what this means for distribution and pricing.

⚖️ California Proposes AI Disclosure Rules for Legal Filings

A new bill introduced in Sacramento would require attorneys to disclose when AI tools were used in drafting any court filing. It’s modeled on the disclosure rules several federal judges have already imposed, but this would be the first state-level statutory requirement. Read our compliance guide.

📊 Solo Practitioners Are Adopting AI Faster Than BigLaw

New ABA survey data shows that solo and small-firm lawyers are adopting AI tools at nearly twice the rate of AmLaw 200 firms. The reason isn’t surprising: they don’t have procurement committees. When one person is the decision-maker, trials start the same day.

Tool Spotlight

Document Diff Tool

Comparing contract redlines manually is medieval. Our free Doc Diff tool highlights every change between two document versions — insertions, deletions, and reformatted clauses — in seconds. No upload to third-party servers required.

Try Doc Diff Free →

That’s it for Week 1. Reply to this email if you’ve got a story, a tool worth reviewing, or a hot take — we read every reply.
